credit-card.notes: BC's 2-year basic limitation period applies to credit card debts. The clock starts on the date you last made a payment, acknowledged the debt in writing, or the debt became due. Making even a small payment can restart the clock.

medical.notes: Private medical debts are subject to BC's 2-year limitation period. Note that MSP-related debts to the provincial government may have different collection rules.

auto-loan.notes: Auto loan deficiency balances follow BC's 2-year limitation period from the date the deficiency arises after vehicle repossession.

personal-loan.notes: Personal loan debts in BC are subject to the standard 2-year limitation period.

student-loan.notes: Private BC student loans follow the 2-year limitation. Provincial StudentAid BC loans and federal loans may have different timelines.

mortgage-deficiency.notes: Mortgage deficiency claims in BC are subject to the 2-year limitation period under the 2012 Limitation Act. There is also a 15-year ultimate limitation period.
mortgage-deficiency.citation: Limitation Act, SBC 2012, c 13, s 6 and s 21

utility-bill.notes: Utility bills are subject to BC's standard 2-year limitation period.

complaints: File a complaint with Consumer Protection BC at consumerprotectionbc.ca or call 1-888-564-9963. Collection agencies are licensed under the Business Practices and Consumer Protection Act, SBC 2004, c 2.
